reasoning: The baseline value as of 2026-04-12 is likely to refer to the unusually high 2026-04-08 observation of 3.183 trillion (up from 3.064T the prior week). The primary immediate driver is the April 15 tax season, which is expected to swell the Treasury General Account (TGA) by over $100B-$185B by late April, correspondingly draining reserves. This makes an increase over the baseline highly unlikely for the April 19 and May 12 resolution dates. By July 11, the tax effect will dissipate, moving the probability closer to 50/50. By October 9, the Federal Reserve's active Reserve Management Purchases (RMPs), initiated in December 2025 to expand the balance sheet, should structurally push reserves upward, justifying a probability above 0.5. For the long-dated horizons from 1 to 10 years out (2027-2036), systemic and macroeconomic uncertainty grows significantly due to unpredictable future monetary regimes, structural inflation, and fiscal policies. Per the instructions, the probabilities for these distant dates progressively regress towards 0.5 as the forecast horizon expands.
